response.justification: Candidate has genuine geospatial/GIS experience (GISCollective, Green Map) and low-latency systems work at Sociomantic, which aligns with the routing pipeline domain. However, modern C++ experience is limited to a ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015, with no Rust experience. Recent roles (Google, Meta, RIDE) are predominantly TypeScript/Node.js/web-stack work, which is a substantial mismatch for a systems-level C++ tile pipeline role. DLang background suggests aptitude for systems programming, but a transition would be needed.
strengths: Direct geospatial/GIS platform experience (GISCollective, Open Green Map)
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work at Sociomantic (millions of req/sec)
Polyglot adaptability and strong testing/TDD discipline
concerns: Modern C++ experience is dated (2014-2015) and brief; no Rust
No navigation/routing systems or map-tile pipeline experience
Recent 5+ years focused on web stack (TS/Node), not systems programming
